title: 「In order to + 動詞」句型
titleZh: 「In order to + 動詞」表達目的
titleEn: Using in order to + verb to express purpose

source.file: Sentence Structure 53 - 「In order to + 動詞」表達目的.pdf
omissions: English versions of Chinese-only Important Rules and Benefits explanations are faithful editorial translations.

formula: Subject + main action + in order to + base verb + other information

formulas.labelZh: 目的放在主要動作之後
formulas.formula: Subject + main action + in order to + base verb + other information
In order to + base verb + other information, + subject + main action
In order not to + base verb

example: I downloaded a financial management app on my phone in order to spend my money wisely.
exampleZh: 我在手機下載了一個理財應用程式，以便更明智地花錢。

examples.en: I downloaded a financial management app on my phone in order to spend my money wisely.
examples.zh: 我在手機下載了一個理財應用程式，以便更明智地花錢。
examples.highlight: in order to spend my money wisely

examples.en: In order to spend my money wisely, I downloaded a financial management app on my phone.
examples.zh: 為了更明智地花錢，我在手機下載了一個理財應用程式。
examples.highlight: In order to spend my money wisely

examples.en: In order not to wake the baby, David spoke quietly.
examples.zh: 為免吵醒嬰兒，大衛輕聲說話。

meaning.zh: In order to + 動詞用來明確說明採取一個行動的目的；目的可以放在主要動作之後或句首。

rules.en: Use the base form of the verb after to.
rules.zh: To 後面使用動詞原形。
examples: in order to improve my English

rules.en: In order to can appear at the beginning of the sentence or after the main action.
rules.zh: In order to 可以放在句首或主要動作之後；放在句首時，目的更加突出。
examples: In order to catch the bus, Lily got up early.
Lily got up early in order to catch the bus.

rules.en: When the purpose phrase begins the sentence, place a comma after it.
rules.zh: 目的部分放在句首時，後面要加逗號。
examples: In order to stay healthy, Ben exercises regularly.

rules.en: The person performing both actions is normally the same. If not, use a structure such as in order to help or allow someone to act.
rules.zh: 進行兩個動作的人通常必須相同；如不同，可使用 in order to help／allow someone 等結構。
examples: In order to catch the first bus, Lily got up early.

rules.en: Use in order not to + base verb for a negative purpose.
rules.zh: 否定目的使用 in order not to + 動詞原形。
examples: In order not to wake the baby, David spoke quietly.

rules.en: Both to and in order to express purpose; in order to is more explicit and slightly more formal.
rules.zh: 普通 to 和 in order to 都可表達目的；in order to 更明確、稍為正式，並可置於句首突出目的。
examples: I go to school to learn something challenging.
I go to school in order to learn something challenging.

benefits.en: It clearly explains why an action is taken.
benefits.zh: 把行動和目的直接連接，讓讀者立即明白採取行動的原因。

benefits.en: It can place the most important purpose first.
benefits.zh: 當目的比行動本身更重要時，可把 In order to 放在句首先突出目標。

benefits.en: It makes a longer explanation more direct.
benefits.zh: 可省去 because I wanted to 或 so that I could 等較長說法。

benefits.en: It varies sentence openings in essays and reports.
benefits.zh: 把 In order to 放在句首可改變句子排列，避免文章聽起來過於重複。
In order not to 可明確說明某個行動是為了防止甚麼事情發生。

sourceOmissions: English versions of Chinese-only Important Rules and Benefits explanations are faithful editorial translations.

en: Rewrite each sentence using in order to or in order not to to show its purpose.
Keep the original meaning unchanged and follow the word order indicated by the first-word hint.
The first word of each answer has been provided.
zh: 使用 in order to 或 in order not to 改寫以下句子，以表達目的。
答案必須保留原句意思，並按照首字提示使用合適的句子次序。
每題已提供答案的第一個字。

questions.prompt: I went to the library because I wanted to borrow a book.
questions.promptZh: 我去了圖書館，因為我想借一本書。
questions.answer: I went to the library in order to borrow a book.
questions.answerZh: 我去了圖書館，目的是借一本書。
questions.highlight: I went to the library in order to borrow a book.

questions.prompt: Lily got up early so that she could catch the first bus.
questions.promptZh: 莉莉早起，以便趕上第一班巴士。
questions.answer: Lily got up early in order to catch the first bus.
questions.answerZh: 莉莉早起，為了趕上第一班巴士。
questions.highlight: Lily got up early in order to catch the first bus.

questions.prompt: Tom called his teacher because he needed to ask a question.
questions.promptZh: 湯姆打電話給老師，因為他需要問一個問題。
questions.answer: Tom called his teacher in order to ask a question.
questions.answerZh: 湯姆打電話給老師，目的是問一個問題。
questions.highlight: Tom called his teacher in order to ask a question.

questions.prompt: We visited the supermarket because we wanted to buy some fresh fruit.
questions.promptZh: 我們去了超級市場，因為我們想買一些新鮮水果。
questions.answer: We visited the supermarket in order to buy some fresh fruit.
questions.answerZh: 我們去了超級市場，為了買一些新鮮水果。
questions.highlight: We visited the supermarket in order to buy some fresh fruit.

questions.prompt: Mia opened the window so that she could get some fresh air.

questions.answer: Mia opened the window in order to get some fresh air.
questions.answerZh: 米婭打開窗戶，為了呼吸一些新鮮空氣。
questions.highlight: Mia opened the window in order to get some fresh air.

questions.prompt: Jack is saving money. His goal is to buy a new bicycle.

questions.answer: Jack is saving money in order to buy a new bicycle.

questions.highlight: Ben visited the doctor in order to get some advice.

questions.prompt: The council expanded the cycling path because it wanted to encourage residents to travel without using cars.
questions.promptZh: 市議會擴建單車徑，因為它希望鼓勵居民不駕車出行。

questions.answer: In order to give students a quiet place to work without being disturbed, the librarian created a quiet study area.
questions.answerZh: 為了讓學生有一個不受打擾的安靜學習空間，圖書館管理員設立了安靜的溫習區。
questions.highlight: In order to give students a quiet place to work without being disturbed, the librarian created a quiet study area.

questions.prompt: The organiser sent everyone a route map in advance because she wanted to prevent participants from getting lost.
questions.promptZh: 主辦人預先向所有人發送路線圖，因為她想防止參加者迷路。
questions.answer: The organiser sent everyone a route map in advance in order to prevent participants from getting lost.
questions.answerZh: 主辦人預先向所有人發送路線圖，以防參加者迷路。
questions.highlight: The organiser sent everyone a route map in advance in order to prevent participants from getting lost.

questions.prompt: The engineers tested the alarm several times because they needed to make sure it would work in an emergency.
questions.promptZh: 工程師測試警報器數次，因為他們需要確保它在緊急情況下能夠運作。
questions.answer: In order to make sure the alarm would work in an emergency, the engineers tested it several times.
questions.answerZh: 為了確保警報器在緊急情況下能夠運作，工程師測試了它數次。
questions.highlight: In order to make sure the alarm would work in an emergency, the engineers tested it several times.

questions.prompt: The customer service team recorded common questions so that new staff could answer customers’ questions more consistently.
questions.promptZh: 顧客服務團隊記錄常見問題，讓新員工可以更一致地回答顧客的問題。
questions.answer: The customer service team recorded common questions in order to help new staff answer customers’ questions more consistently.
questions.answerZh: 顧客服務團隊記錄常見問題，以幫助新員工更一致地回答顧客的問題。
questions.highlight: The customer service team recorded common questions in order to help new staff answer customers’ questions more consistently.

questions.prompt: The local library’s weekend opening hours were extended so that working adults could borrow books more easily.
questions.promptZh: 區內圖書館延長了週末開放時間，讓在職成年人可以更容易借書。
questions.answer: The local library’s weekend opening hours were extended in order to make it easier for working adults to borrow books.
questions.answerZh: 區內圖書館延長了週末開放時間，讓在職成年人可以更容易借書。
questions.highlight: The local library’s weekend opening hours were extended in order to make it easier for working adults to borrow books.
